Importing from qiime2

Source: vignettes/Importing_from_qiime2.Rmd
Importing_from_qiime2.Rmd

strollur includes the function read_qiime2() as well as several functions to read qiime2 output files individually. To create a dataset from the outputs of the qiime2 moving-pictures example, run the following:

qza_files <- c(
  strollur_example("rep_seqs.qza"),
  strollur_example("table.qza"),
  strollur_example("taxonomy.qza"),
  strollur_example("rooted-tree.qza")
)

data <- read_qiime2(
  qza = qza_files,
  metadata = strollur_example("sample_metadata.tsv"),
  dataset_name = "qiime_moving_pictures"
)
#> ℹ Added metadata.
#> ℹ Added 759 sequences.
#> ℹ Assigned 759 sequence abundances.
#> ℹ Assigned 759 asv bins.
#> ℹ Assigned 759 asv bin taxonomies.

To view a summary of data:

data
#> qiime_moving_pictures:
#> 
#>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs
#> Minimum:         1  120    120      0        3     0      1.00
#> 2.5%-tile:       1  120    120      0        3     0   3933.45
#> 25%-tile:        1  120    120      0        4     0  39325.50
#> Median:          1  120    120      0        4     0  78650.00
#> 75%-tile:        1  120    120      0        4     0 117974.50
#> 97.5%-tile:      1  120    120      0        6     0 153366.55
#> Maximum:         1  120    120      0        8     0 157298.00
#> Mean:            1  120    120      0        4     0      0.00
#> 
#> Number of unique seqs: 759 
#> Total number of seqs: 157298 
#> 
#> Total number of samples: 34 
#> Total number of asvs: 759 
#> Your dataset includes metadata

Reading Individual Files

  • unpack_qiime2_artifact() unpacks qiime2 qza files and returns an artifact
  • read_qiime2_feature_table() reads a qiime2 qza file containing bin data
  • read_qiime2_taxonomy() reads a qiime2 qza file containing containing taxonomy data
  • read_qiime2_metadata() read qiime2 tsv table containing metadata

To create a dataset and read the individual files, you can use the functions below. First let’s create a dataset named my_data.

my_data <- new_dataset(dataset_name = "my_data")

To decompress the individual qza files, you can use the unpack_qiime2_artifact(). unpack_qiime2_artifact will extract a folder containing the data, versioning and provenance information. Let’s take a look at the rep_seqs.qza file first.

fasta_artifact <- unpack_qiime2_artifact(qza = strollur_example("rep_seqs.qza"))

fasta_artifact is a list. To add the FASTA data to your dataset you can use the read_fasta() function:

fasta_file <- file.path(
  getwd(), "rep_seqs", fasta_artifact$uuid, "data",
  "dna-sequences.fasta"
)
fasta_data <- read_fasta(fasta = fasta_file)

fasta_data is a data.frame containing sequence names, sequence nucleotide strings, and comments if provided. You can add the FASTA sequences to your dataset using the add() function:

add(
  my_data,
  table = fasta_data,
  type = "sequences"
)
#> ℹ Added 759 sequences.
#> [1] 759

You can remove the fasta_artifact using the unlink function:

unlink(file.path(getwd(), "rep_seqs"), recursive = TRUE)

Now that we have added the FASTA sequences, let’s read the abundance data found in the table.qza file. Since we know that the sequences are in fact features, we will assign the abundances to the sequences and then we can assign the sequences to bins to create Amplicon Sequence Variant asv clusters. Let’s use the read_qiime2_feature_table() function to extract the abundance table.

abundance <- read_qiime2_feature_table(strollur_example("table.qza"))

assign(
  my_data,
  table = abundance$data,
  type = "sequence_abundance",
  table_names = list(sequence_name = "bin_names")
)
#> ℹ Assigned 759 sequence abundances.
#> [1] 759

assign(
  my_data,
  table = abundance$data,
  type = "bins",
  bin_type = "asv",
  table_names = list(sequence_name = "bin_names")
)
#> ℹ Assigned 759 asv bins.
#> [1] 759

my_data
#> my_data:
#> 
#>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs
#> Minimum:         1  120    120      0        3     0      1.00
#> 2.5%-tile:       1  120    120      0        3     0   3933.45
#> 25%-tile:        1  120    120      0        4     0  39325.50
#> Median:          1  120    120      0        4     0  78650.00
#> 75%-tile:        1  120    120      0        4     0 117974.50
#> 97.5%-tile:      1  120    120      0        6     0 153366.55
#> Maximum:         1  120    120      0        8     0 157298.00
#> Mean:            1  120    120      0        4     0      0.00
#> 
#> Number of unique seqs: 759 
#> Total number of seqs: 157298 
#> 
#> Total number of samples: 34 
#> Total number of asvs: 759

Now that we have added the abundance data and assigned the sequences to bins, let’s take a look at the taxonomic classifications provided in the taxonomy.qza file. We will use the read_qiime2_taxonomy() function to extract the feature classification table.

taxonomy <- read_qiime2_taxonomy(strollur_example("taxonomy.qza"))

assign(
  my_data,
  table = taxonomy$data,
  type = "sequence_taxonomy",
  table_names = list(sequence_name = "bin_names")
)
#> ℹ Assigned 759 sequence taxonomies.
#> [1] 759

Now, lets add a tree that shows the relationships between the sequences (features).

tree_artifact <- unpack_qiime2_artifact(
  qza =
    strollur_example("rooted-tree.qza")
)

We can read the tree using the ape::read.tree() function.

tree_file <- file.path(
  getwd(), "rooted-tree", tree_artifact$uuid, "data",
  "tree.nwk"
)
sequence_tree <- ape::read.tree(tree_file)

You can add the tree to your dataset as follows:

my_data$add_sequence_tree(tree = sequence_tree)

You can remove the tree_artifact using the following:

unlink(file.path(getwd(), "rooted-tree"), recursive = TRUE)

Lastly, let’s read the metadata provided by qiime2 using the read_qiime2_metadata() function.

metadata <- read_qiime2_metadata(
  metadata =
    strollur_example("sample_metadata.tsv")
)

To add the metadata to the my_data dataset, run the following:

add(my_data, table = metadata, type = "metadata")
#> ℹ Added metadata.
#> [1] 1

To view a summary of your imported dataset, ‘my_data’, run the following:

my_data
#> my_data:
#> 
#>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs
#> Minimum:         1  120    120      0        3     0      1.00
#> 2.5%-tile:       1  120    120      0        3     0   3933.45
#> 25%-tile:        1  120    120      0        4     0  39325.50
#> Median:          1  120    120      0        4     0  78650.00
#> 75%-tile:        1  120    120      0        4     0 117974.50
#> 97.5%-tile:      1  120    120      0        6     0 153366.55
#> Maximum:         1  120    120      0        8     0 157298.00
#> Mean:            1  120    120      0        4     0      0.00
#> 
#> Number of unique seqs: 759 
#> Total number of seqs: 157298 
#> 
#> Total number of samples: 34 
#> Total number of asvs: 759 
#> Your dataset includes metadata

Developed by Sarah Westcott.
